title: Crib Mattress Sofa and Sectional
summary: A kids' 2x4 sofa frame sized to a crib mattress cushion, with a separate slatted-support arm piece that ties two sofas into a sectional — build one sofa alone, or two joined by the arm.
description:
This kids' sofa is built entirely from mirror-image 2x4 leg pairs and slatted framing, sized specifically to a standard crib mattress rather than a custom foam cushion — a genuinely economical cushion source for a kids' furniture piece. **Build the two leg sets in mirror image, not identical** — the plan calls this out directly, since pocket holes need to face inward on both sides for the frame's fasteners to stay hidden, which means the two legs are true mirrors of each other, not two copies of the same piece.

Once the leg sets are built, horizontal supports and a back top support tie them into one sofa frame, and the slats attach inside the frame last, screwed at the frame and also into the last slat at each end for extra rack resistance. **A single sofa stands complete on its own** — the arm piece is a genuinely separate addition, built only if you want to join two sofas into an L-shaped sectional or want an armrest divider.

The arm uses the same pocket-hole construction as the sofa frames and, once attached, ties two sofa units together while also supporting their backrests — a real structural role, not just a decorative divider between two seats.

materials.name: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

steps.title: Cut all the parts
steps.body: Cut two back legs (per sofa) at 26", two side apron (per sofa) at 23-1/2", two front legs (per sofa) at 12", three front/back rails (per sofa) at 48-1/2", one top back support (per sofa) at 51-1/2", ten slats (per sofa) at 25-1/2", two arm legs at 26", two arm sides at 27-1/2" and one arm top at 30-1/2" from the 2x4, 9 ft (per sofa). Allowing about 1/8" for each saw cut, that comes to eight 9 ft 2x4, 9 ft (per sofa). Square every cut and label each piece as it comes off the saw — several parts here are within half an inch of each other and are easy to mix up later.

steps.title: Build the leg sets
steps.body: Drill two 1-1/2" pocket holes on each end of the side apron boards. Attach to the front and back legs with 2-1/2" pocket hole screws and glue. Build two leg sets in MIRROR image (pocket holes on the inside of each).

steps.title: Join the leg sets
steps.body: With pocket holes on the inside, attach the horizontal front/back rails to the leg sets with pocket holes.

steps.title: Attach the back top support
steps.body: Attach the back top support to the legs with 2-1/2" screws, predrilling holes to prevent splitting.

steps.title: Add the slats
steps.body: Drill two 1-1/2" pocket holes on each end of the slat boards. Attach inside the frame. Also screw the sides of the sofa to the last slats at each end for added structure.

steps.title: Build the arm (optional, for sectional)
steps.body: Build the arm using 1-1/2" pocket holes and 2-1/2" pocket hole screws and glue. Attach the arm between two sofas to tie them together and support the backrests.

steps.title: Finish
steps.body: Sand all surfaces and apply primer and paint, or stain and poly. Add the crib mattress as the seat cushion.
materials: Primer and paint, or stain and poly

images.alt: Kids' 2x4 sofa sized to a crib mattress cushion
